siteFooter.tagline: Un protocolo de mundo voxel con semillas en Solana.

hero.title: A fair voxel world must publish its rules before it asks for trust.
hero.subtitle: NiceChunk documents the code path from seed to chunk, from player action to state change, and from client behavior to future on-chain verification.

protocolMap.title: The docs follow the same layers as the product.
protocolMap.body: NiceChunk is not documented as a marketing site. It is documented as a public rules surface for players, builders, node operators, auditors, and future governance.

world.title: World Protocol
world.body: Seeded terrain, large biome masks, slope-limited mountains, oceans, forests, snow bands, and block resource rules.

actions.title: Action Rules
actions.body: Hotbar-selected behavior, mining range, block placement, inventory movement, forging inputs, and validation boundaries.

verification.title: State & PDA Model
verification.body: GlobalConfig, player sessions, chunk-broken records, resource-drop tables, Guardian registry PDAs, version hashes, and what should not be stored on-chain.

network.title: Guardian Network
network.body: Regional access nodes, realtime messages, non-custodial boundaries, NCK stake, and future availability proofs.

assets.title: On-chain Asset Research
assets.body: NCM stores compact voxel assets as chain text; NCFM explores Fourier/function payloads that can regenerate 3D items from tiny records.

civilization.title: Civilization Layer
civilization.body: Natural Law, Civilization Law, participation power, rule books, validator checks, cooldowns, and bounded configuration execution.

statusModel.title: Every rule must say whether it is live, planned, client-only, or intended for chain verification.
statusModel.body: This is the core documentation rule for NiceChunk. A feature can be playable before it is fully decentralized, but the page must say which part is already live and which part is still a protocol target.

live.label: Live Alpha
live.title: Playable client behavior
live.body: Browser world, seeded terrain, movement, mining interaction, placement preview, hotbar, backpack UI, forging UI, minimap, and wallet login.

chain.label: Devnet / Chain
chain.title: Explicit Solana state
chain.body: GlobalConfig, player/session bridges, Guardian registry and region accounts, NCK mint binding, and PDA derivation rules.

planned.label: Planned Protocol
planned.title: Future verification layers
planned.body: Placed-block overrides, richer resource settlement, civilization rule execution, Guardian challenges, and long-term governance controls.

boundary.label: Trust Boundary
boundary.title: No hidden claims
boundary.body: The docs must not imply that client-only rendering, local cache, or unreleased systems are already fully decentralized.

implementation.title: Current docs should describe the game that actually exists today.
implementation.body: This snapshot separates live browser behavior, devnet-backed state, and protocol targets so players can understand what is authoritative now and what still belongs to the roadmap.

world.title: Infinite seeded world
world.body: Terrain is generated on demand from deterministic rules. The client loads nearby chunks, keeps discovered map information, and unloads render resources outside the active area.

water.title: Water as blocks
water.body: Rivers, lakes, oceans, and dynamic water visuals use water block entries. Water is shorter than a normal block, ignored by collision, blocks mining selection above covered cells, and uses depth-based color.

player.title: Player and backpack PDAs
player.body: PlayerProfile stores public player state and the equipped backpack address. Backpack contents live in a v2 Backpack account; rewarded mining appends block resource records through the Backpack program after a valid player session.

guardian.title: Guardian access layer
guardian.body: The browser can connect to Guardian realtime endpoints for nearby player movement, chat, and dig events. Guardians provide access and visibility; they do not custody assets or sign player transactions.

quickStart.title: Enter, inspect, and verify the alpha world.

enter.title: Enter the world
enter.body: Open the browser client, connect a Solana wallet, and create a player profile before moving into the voxel world.

explore.title: Explore chunks
explore.body: Walk through seeded terrain while the client loads nearby chunks, caches explored map data, and unloads GPU resources when regions are far away.

act.title: Use the selected item
act.body: The hotbar controls the action: empty hand swings, pickaxe mines, and block items place blocks within range.

worldGeneration.title: The same seed and rule version must rebuild the same world.
worldGeneration.body: Terrain is generated from deterministic inputs. Large climate regions are sampled first, then oceans, dunes, foothills, mountains, snow bands, trees, and resource hints are layered on top.

seed.title: Seed contract
seed.body: A public seed is normalized into numeric streams. Terrain, climate, tree palettes, and resource hints must use seed-derived streams.

slope.title: Slope-limited mountains
slope.body: Mountain growth is broad and probabilistic. Final terrain limits neighboring columns to natural one-block steps where gameplay requires traversal.

biomes.title: Large biome regions
biomes.body: Low-frequency climate masks allow large plains, deserts, ocean bodies, forests, foothills, and snow regions without hard size caps.

waterSystem.title: Water is a block state first, then a visual surface.
waterSystem.body: The terrain generator and dynamic water layer treat water as cell data. Rendering can make water translucent and shorter, but gameplay checks must derive from block state instead of screen pixels.

generated.title: Generated water blocks
generated.body: Sea, lake, and river cells resolve to water-family block ids. Their top should never render above the surrounding ground plane.

collision.title: No solid collision
collision.body: Player collision ignores water cells so the character naturally falls to the real solid block underneath instead of standing on water.

selection.title: Water blocks mining
selection.body: If water covers a target column, mining selection is blocked. This prevents players from standing in water and mining hidden blocks underneath.

chunkSystem.title: World coordinates become chunk coordinates, local block coordinates, and cache keys.
chunkSystem.body: A chunk is a 16 x 16 X/Z area. The client currently renders a 13 x 13 visible chunk square and preloads a wider 19 x 19 square to reduce stalls. Rendering resources can unload while discovered minimap data stays cached.

chainState.title: The chain stores commitments and player-owned state, not every generated block.
chainState.body: NiceChunk’s storage model is designed around one rule: deterministic generated terrain should be free to reconstruct, while changed or economically meaningful state becomes explicit and verifiable.

rows.global: Immutable world-law layer: NCK mint, world seed hash, chunk size, rule hashes, fees, and Guardian parameters.
rows.player: Public player account with owner, global config, position, visible equipment, backpack style, and equipped backpack address.
rows.session: Short-lived authority account used so gameplay actions can be submitted without asking the wallet to sign every mined block.
rows.backpack: Player-owned inventory account. It stores capacity, item count, carried state, and mined resource coordinate records.
rows.chunkBroken: Compact mined-block record for broken generated cells. It avoids writing full generated terrain to chain storage.
rows.registry: Registry for active Guardian regions, NCK stake amount, treasury token account, and registration counters.
rows.region: A registered region endpoint with owner, operator, host, TLS flag, stake, proof counters, and update slot.
rows.chunk: Future production account for placed block deltas and current block overrides only. Unchanged generated terrain should not be stored.
rows.resourceDropTable: Bounded extra-drop rule table used by rewarded mining. Each rule names source block, drop block, chance, altitude, depth, and salt.
rows.recipeTable: Smelting recipe table with authority, recipe ids, heat tiers, input slot records, output slot records, and updated slot.
rows.asset: Compact owned item or model record with category, quantity, item hash, item code, durability, and bounded payload bytes.
rows.listing: Marketplace listing state for backpack resources or asset PDAs, including seller, price, currency, escrow source, buyer, and sold/canceled state.

playerBackpack.title: Buying a backpack and equipping a backpack are separate state changes.
playerBackpack.body: The Backpack program creates the inventory account. The Player program binds that account to the player's public profile. The profile panel only displays a backpack after the equipped backpack field in PlayerProfile points to a valid Backpack account owned by the same wallet.

steps.player: Create PlayerProfile if it does not exist.
steps.create: Create Backpack PDA with seed ["backpack", owner, backpack_id].
steps.bind: Call Player program instruction 5 to write the Backpack public key into PlayerProfile.
steps.verify: Reload PlayerProfile, then load the referenced Backpack and verify both accounts share the same owner.
steps.session: Create or refresh a PlayerSession before submitting break/place actions through a session authority.

performance.title: The infinite world stays cheap by deriving first and storing only changes.
performance.body: NiceChunk should not pay per generated block and should not add per-frame logic for rules that can be resolved at generation, chunk-build, click, or transaction time.

render.title: Batch by chunk
render.body: Blocks, water, trees, and decorations are built into chunk meshes or instanced batches. Far chunks use cheaper detail modes instead of per-object realtime decisions.

algorithm.title: No visual detection for logic
algorithm.body: Collision, water coverage, mining selection, and path checks should use generated block state and local deltas, not browser screenshots or pixel inspection.

storage.title: Sparse changed cells
storage.body: The default world is reconstructed from seed. PDA data should hold compact deltas, mined masks, or section patches only where players changed the world.

water.title: Dynamic water stays bounded
water.body: MVP flowing water is visual or client-side. Future authoritative water should store compact source changes, not full flowing grids.

playerActions.title: The selected hotbar slot is the action contract.
playerActions.body: NiceChunk actions should be predictable before they are submitted. The client resolves input based on the held item, then range and target rules decide whether an action is valid.

empty.title: Empty hand
empty.body: Clicking with no held item produces a swing only. It does not mine or place.

pickaxe.title: Pickaxe
pickaxe.body: A mining tool resolves clicks into mining attempts and consumes durability when successful.

block.title: Block item
block.body: A block stack resolves clicks into placement attempts within range and consumes one item per successful placement.

mining.title: Resource outcomes should be reproducible from public inputs.
mining.body: Current mining verifies generated block identity from GlobalConfig and chunk coordinates, rejects air, water, bedrock, and already-mined cells, records the broken cell, then can append the mined block and one bounded extra drop into the equipped backpack.

forging.title: Items need readable state before they become tradable assets.
forging.body: Hotbar slots, durability, stack limits, smelting recipe tables, material inputs, output records, and future asset metadata are documented as state models rather than hidden UI behavior.

hotbar.title: Hotbar
hotbar.body: The active bar has 9 slots. Stackable items can hold up to 99 per slot.

durability.title: Durability
durability.body: Tools use durability instead of stack amount. The current default tool durability is 999.

recipes.title: Smelting recipes
recipes.body: RecipeTable accounts store enabled recipes with min heat tier, up to 8 input slot records, up to 4 output slot records, and an authority that can be transferred.

assetsMarket.title: Tradable items need compact asset data and explicit listing state.
assetsMarket.body: NiceChunk has separate workstreams for compact on-chain 3D assets, CPU-searchable asset genes, and marketplace listings. These systems should remain clearly separated from raw gameplay inventory until ownership and settlement rules are complete.

ncm.title: NCM asset format
ncm.body: NCM converts voxel models into compact blockchain-readable text so clients can reconstruct 3D assets from account data without an external gateway.

dna.title: NCM DNA search
dna.body: NCM DNA explores CPU-friendly searches for shorter deterministic genes that reproduce the same asset hash and reduce future PDA storage size.

market.title: Market listings
market.body: The market program creates seller-owned listing PDAs, can cancel active listings, and can buy listings with SOL or NCK while routing a 1% market fee.

ncfm.title: NCFM / UPOW research
ncfm.body: NCFM explores function-defined assets and a useful-computation path where search work can discover shorter payloads that still reproduce a verified model.

economy.title: NCK utility must be documented by status, not by hype.
economy.body: NCK is designed as the core utility token for access, in-world actions, forging systems, marketplace settlement, Guardian incentives, and future governance mechanics.

genesis.notes: Access can be connected to NCK and Genesis Pass logic.

forging.notes: Economic settlement should expose recipe and material state.

market.notes: Player-owned assets require explicit asset metadata and settlement rules.

guardian.notes: Guardian registration currently sends 100,000 NCK to the treasury token account on devnet.

governance.notes: Protocol parameter changes should be versioned and reviewable.

smelting.notes: Recipe tables are on-chain state; recipe authority remains explicit until a future civilization config path exists.

watchers.title: Guardians and Guardians are access infrastructure, not asset custodians.
watchers.body: The current Guardian program registers region endpoints and NCK stake on devnet. The broader Guardian network concept is the realtime access layer that keeps regions reachable without owning player assets or signing player transactions.

noCustody.title: No custody
noCustody.body: Guardians do not hold player assets and do not replace wallet authorization.

noTransactions.title: No transaction signing
noTransactions.body: Players interact with Solana wallets directly. Guardians should not process transactions as hidden owners.

registry.title: Guardian registry
registry.body: GuardianRegion accounts store 100 x 100 chunk regions, endpoint, owner, operator, stake, proof counters, penalties, and update slot.

magicblock.title: High-frequency chunk changes should move through a bounded realtime path.
magicblock.body: The MagicBlock Ephemeral Rollups bridge is scoped to one active chunk first. It is a devnet-only direction for fast break/place updates, not a replacement for wallet ownership or final settlement.

singleChunk.title: Single active chunk
singleChunk.body: The client should subscribe to the player's current chunk, then unsubscribe and resubscribe when the player crosses chunk boundaries.

session.title: PlayerSession authority
session.body: A wallet signs once to create or refresh a session PDA. The temporary session key can then sign allowed gameplay actions until expiry.

noPolling.title: No broad RPC polling
noPolling.body: The design should not re-enable wide multi-chunk polling on public RPC. Realtime updates must stay bounded to active interest areas.

notProd.title: Not production enabled
notProd.body: Do not enable the browser ER switch for production until the chunk program is redeployed and a chunk delegation is verified end to end.

solana.title: Wallet identity and protocol state should stay explicit.
solana.body: The docs separate live wallet flows, devnet programs, local client cache, and future mainnet commitments. This prevents alpha client behavior from being confused with finalized protocol guarantees.

debugging.title: A signature is not enough; confirmed transactions must be checked for errors.
debugging.body: Wallets can return a signature even when the confirmed transaction later contains an error. Client code and support docs should verify confirmation status, account ownership, and PDA fields before telling a player that state is bound.

wallet.title: Wallet mismatch
wallet.body: The connected provider must match the stored NiceChunk wallet address. Multiple installed wallets can expose different providers.

confirm.title: Check value.err
confirm.body: After confirmTransaction, inspect the returned error field before treating a purchase, listing, mining action, or session refresh as successful.

pda.title: Read the authoritative PDA
pda.body: For backpacks, read PlayerProfile first. The profile must point to a Backpack account whose owner equals the wallet address.

fairness.title: Rules are only fair when players can inspect the rulebook.
fairness.body: NiceChunk should keep deterministic rules public, version major rule changes, publish test vectors, disclose trust limits, distinguish live features from planned protocol mechanics, and route future civilization changes through explicit config accounts.

deterministic.title: Deterministic generation
deterministic.body: The same seed, chunk coordinate, and generation version should produce the same result.

versioned.title: Versioned rule changes
versioned.body: UI changes, world-rule changes, economy changes, and chain-state changes should be tracked separately.

limits.title: Trust limits
limits.body: The docs must state what is client-only, what is Guardian-assisted, and what is intended for on-chain verification.

civilization.title: Civilization boundary
civilization.body: Civilization can evolve configuration only after explicit validation and thresholds; it is not a project-side text command that rewrites ownership or core code.

changelog.title: Protocol documentation must record what changed and what did not.

v0163.title: Protocol docs expanded
v0163.body: Adds current implementation snapshot, water block rules, player/backpack binding flow, expanded PDA account coverage, storage principles, market scope, MagicBlock ER boundaries, and transaction debugging.

v0150.title: Docs alpha introduced
v0150.body: Adds protocol docs page, public architecture, demos, independent docs language resources, and mainnet docs locale indexing.

v0148.title: Homepage hero logo size update
v0148.body: UI-only change. No gameplay, world generation, economy, or chain-state rule changes.

v0164.title: Docs aligned with current programs
v0164.body: Documents resource-drop tables, rewarded mining, v2 backpack storage, smelting recipe execution, marketplace buy flow, and the civilization governance boundary.

civilizationDocs.title: Civilization lets players write the world's laws without giving the project hidden authority.
civilizationDocs.body: The Civilization Codex is NiceChunk's governance design surface. It separates world-level Natural Law from evolving Civilization Law, uses participation-based power instead of raw wallet count, and routes approved changes into bounded configuration PDAs rather than secret admin overrides.

natural.title: Natural Law
natural.body: Natural Law protects the deepest fairness assumptions of the world. It requires stronger prompts, stricter validators, longer cooling, broader challenge windows, and 90% world-level agreement.

civilization.title: Civilization Law
civilization.body: Civilization Law lets players evolve production, construction, craft, resource rules, and social configuration with a lower threshold while still staying inside validator-checked patch limits.

power.title: Participation-weighted citizens
power.body: Power should come from real participation in the world, constrained by maturity, caps, active windows, decay, and anomaly penalties so governance is not reduced to wallet count or raw NCK balance.

execution.title: Permissionless execution
execution.body: After threshold, cooling, and challenge conditions are satisfied, any player should be able to call execute_rule. The contract checks on-chain state and writes approved configuration only.

modules.title: Target configuration modules
modules.body: The public Codex names smelting, forging, block_properties, resource_rules, element_rules, and world_knowledge as future module targets.

boundary.title: Mock UI today, contract path tomorrow
boundary.body: The current Civilization page publishes mock rule books and future account structure. No deployed civilization program currently mutates gameplay PDAs.

ncmDocs.title: NCM makes the 3D asset itself part of readable protocol state.
ncmDocs.body: The NCM page documents NiceChunk's compact voxel asset format. A MagicaVoxel .vox file is parsed locally, same-color voxels are merged into cuboids, and the result becomes NCM2 text that can live in account data or a program-owned asset record. A client can fetch it through normal RPC and rebuild the model without IPFS, mutable JSON, or a private asset server.

format.title: PDA-friendly text
format.body: NCM2 records encode palette, box count, signed positions, dimensions, and model hash as compact text so explorers and game clients can inspect the asset directly.

converter.title: Browser-local VOX converter
converter.body: The public converter supports MagicaVoxel SIZE, XYZI, and RGBA chunks, previews the model, then exports an NCM payload ready for account storage experiments.

compression.title: Hundreds of bytes, not asset hosting
compression.body: The current examples compress 23 KB class-sized VOX characters into roughly 354-452 byte NCM records, which is the difference between on-chain state and external file dependency.

dna.title: NCM-DNA continuation
dna.body: NCM-DNA continues the same idea by searching for deterministic short genes that reproduce the same model hash, turning asset compression into a useful computation problem.

ncfmDocs.title: NCFM turns a 3D item into a deterministic function payload.
ncfmDocs.body: NCFM means NiceChunk Fourier Model. Instead of storing a mesh file or a voxel list, it stores a tiny canonical record: seed, quantized coefficients, palette rules, grid limits, and a verifier hash. The client samples the agreed functions and reconstructs the voxel item. This is the UPOW direction: useful work should improve asset representation, not burn compute on meaningless hashes.

payload.title: Function as asset identity
payload.body: The public NCFM lab demonstrates a compact payload such as NCFM1.PKX.S37x39x9.FXY:dseg+harm12.FZ:thick7.PAL:g0.6.H:9bd2a6, about 214 bytes for the example item.

sampler.title: FXY x FZ reconstruction
sampler.body: The lab shows an XY Fourier outline intersected with a Z thickness function. Coordinates accepted by both functions become material voxels.

hash.title: Same payload, same model hash
hash.body: Canonical bounds, coefficient quantization, palette, sampling order, and hash rules let any client or explorer verify that the visible object matches the chain payload.

boundary.title: Research direction, not settlement yet
boundary.body: NCFM and UPOW are documented as the next asset-computation layer. They are not presented as an already deployed settlement contract in the current mainnet product.

thesis.title: NiceChunk is a public world protocol, not only a voxel client.
thesis.body: The project is built around one principle: every important world rule, asset identity, player action, and future governance path should be inspectable, reproducible, and eventually executable through explicit protocol state.

world.title: Deterministic world
world.body: Terrain, resources, water, trees, snow, and chunk boundaries come from public rules so the world can be regenerated and audited instead of trusted as server output.

state.title: State is explicit
state.body: The docs separate generated defaults from player deltas, PDA accounts, local cache, devnet contracts, and future mainnet commitments.

assets.title: Assets belong to the protocol
assets.body: NCM and NCFM push 3D item identity toward chain-readable data and deterministic reconstruction instead of external files that can disappear or change.

upow.title: Computation should be useful
upow.body: NCM-DNA and NCFM/UPOW turn search work toward shorter asset genes or better function payloads that reproduce a verified model hash.

civilization.title: Civilization writes law
civilization.body: The long-term governance model is not hidden admin control. It is readable rule books, participation power, cooldowns, challenges, and bounded config execution.

guardian.title: Access without custody
guardian.body: Guardians should keep regions reachable and realtime, but wallets keep authority and contracts keep settlement boundaries explicit.
